div1.entryFree:
Glow, vb. 1) intr. to shine with heat without a flame: coals of --ing fire, Ven. 35. Wiv. III, 5, 122. Mids. V, 382. Per. I, 2, 41. Metaphorically, == to be hot, to burn: Lucr. 47. Sonn. 73, 9. Cor. IV, 3, 26. == to be red: --ing roses, Compl. 286. == to become red with animation: Ven. 337. Compl. 324. John IV, 1, 114. Caes. I, 2, 183. Hml. III, 4, 48. Per. V, 1, 96. == to be bright, to shine: his eyes that have --ed like plated Mars, Ant. I, 1, 4.
2) trans. to make hot, to flush: to g. the delicate cheeks, Ant. II, 2, 209.
